title: lupinus leucophyllus subsp. erectus
intro: Lupinus leucophyllus subsp. erectus, also known as the Big Bend lupine, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the legume family (Fabaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to produce isoflavones, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The isoflavones present in the plant are believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing the risk of heart disease and certain types of cancer, as well as alleviating symptoms of menopause and osteoporosis. Lupinus leucophyllus subsp. erectus is traditionally prepared as a tea or capsule, with dried root and stem being used in herbal medicine.
